Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 09 Aug 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Recap: Discovery — Silverline Pay pilot & cost levers

Elena / Raj — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our discovery + clear next steps so we’re aligned.

What we heard (pain / goals)
- Volume profile: ~200–350M tokens/month in Q1 (peak in fraud review flows).
- Latency SLA: 250–400ms p95 for user-facing scoring; more tolerant for async reconciliation.
- Budget posture: constrained pilot budget (~$50–80k/month cap) — procurement needs a clear ROI and predictable TCO.
- Data controls: PII redaction + audit logs required; likely Private or Dedicated if we move past pilot.

Primary success criteria for pilot (agreed)...

Date: Fri, 7 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Draft SLA and questions on monthly remedy formula

Jordan —

Thanks for sending the initial template. Attached is Synapse's procurement markup and a short list of questions we need clarified before procurement can move to PO. High-level items:

1) Uptime: We're asking for a 99.95% monthly availability commitment for our production cluster (multi-AZ).
2) Remedy: Synapse prefers a pro-rata monthly credit tied to % downtime rather than a fixed cap; they want clear calculation examples.
3) Maintenance: we need a defined maintenance carve-out + a 72-hour advance notice requirement.
4) Regional failover: how will cross-region failovers count toward SLA?

I've attached Synapse_marked_up_SLA.pdf for reference. Can you confirm...

Samantha

From: Mira Singh <mira@aerosense.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Diego Ramos <diego.ramos@aerosense.ai>
Date: Sat, 25 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Packaging question — feature portability + surge behavior

Camila,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Quick follow-up from our product/ops review: we have two principal asks as we think about a tier move later this quarter.

1) Portability: If we upgrade from Compact to Throughput, can we keep our per-team entitlements and feature flags (reranking, structured-output) active without refactoring prompts or API routes? In particular, we need clarity on whether feature gating lives at the account level or the plan level.

2) Surge handling: During periodic batch jobs we spike for ~30–45 minutes. How does a temporary surge interact with reserved capacity vs. burst credits? Can we run a pilot where we keep our Compact seat count...
